title: SieveError has publicly mutable fields
description: parse_error.rs — SieveError is a public struct with pub fields: message: String and kind: SieveErrorKind. This means any caller can write err.message = String::from("overwritten") or err.kind = SieveErrorKind::Utf8, silently corrupting the error state. For error types, the fields should be private with getter methods (fn message(&self) -> &str, fn kind(&self) -> &SieveErrorKind) so the struct invariants (message consistent with kind, source consistent with kind) cannot be violated from outside the crate. The same applies to ParseError's pub message, pub line, pub col. Public error fields also make semver harder: adding a field to SieveError is a breaking change for struct literal construction.
